message:
feat(frame): support duplicate index labels when all inputs are exactly aligned

Previously, any Series arithmetic or DataFrame concat(axis=1) operation
involving duplicate index labels was unconditionally rejected, even when
all inputs shared the exact same index — the common case in real pandas
workflows (e.g., groupby results with non-unique group labels).

Changes to Series binary operations (add, sub, mul, div, etc.):
- Detect exact_duplicate_fast_path when both series have duplicate labels
  but identical indexes, bypassing the expensive align_union + reindex
  pipeline entirely
- Only reject duplicate labels when indexes actually differ (the case
  where reindex semantics are ambiguous)

Changes to concat_dataframes_axis1:
- Outer join: when all input indexes are identical, skip union
  computation and use the shared index directly, supporting duplicates
- Outer join: when indexes differ, compute deduplicated union preserving
  first-seen label order (matching pandas behavior)
- Inner join: deduplicate the initial frame's labels before intersection
- Reindex phase: fast-path identity mapping when frame index matches
  target; reject with clear error only when actual reindex of duplicate
  labels would be required

Tests added:
- strict_mode_allows_duplicate_indices_when_exactly_aligned: verifies
  Series arithmetic produces correct element-wise results with dup labels
- concat_dataframes_axis1_duplicate_index_outer_exact_match_preserves_rows:
  verifies axis=1 concat with matching duplicate indexes
- concat_dataframes_axis1_duplicate_index_reindex_rejects: renamed for
  clarity, verifies rejection when duplicate indexes differ
- concat_dataframes_axis1_inner_duplicate_index_reindex_rejects: new
  test for inner join rejection case

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
